description_long: This structure is a traditional East Asian pavilion, characterized by its sweeping, upturned eaves and intricate tiled roof. The roof is supported by a series of robust wooden columns, which are evenly spaced along the perimeter. The open sides allow for visibility and airflow, while the lower half features ornate wooden railings with decorative carvings. The roof tiles are arranged in a meticulous, repetitive pattern, giving the building a sense of rhythm and harmony. The color palette is dominated by muted greens and browns, evoking a natural, harmonious aesthetic. This type of building is commonly found in historical parks, gardens, or temple complexes, serving as a place for rest, contemplation, or gatherings.
description: A traditional East Asian pavilion with a green tiled roof, wooden columns, and ornate railings, open on all sides for airflow and visibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the pavilion, with visible wooden columns and detailed railings beneath the expansive tiled roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the curvature of the roof, and the solid end walls with minimal openings.
description_view_2: Rear view nearly identical to the front, displaying the same arrangement of columns and railings under the tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the roof's length and the solid end wall.
